summary: Unmerged WIP attempt to decompile libvector.s into src/lbvector.c. The PR renamed the remaining assembly to asm/lbvector_asm.s, disabled part of it with .if 0/.endif while replacing it with C, added math-related header content, adjusted compiler/linker floating-point flags, split the huge asm/text.s into text_part1-4, and added an asmdiff helper. The author later closed the PR, saying they were giving up because they suspected the compiler version was wrong.

key_files.role: New C implementation attempt for libvector routines; 1259 lines added.
Renamed from asm/lbvector.s; retained remaining assembly and disabled a decompiled region with .if 0/.endif.
Added math declarations or definitions needed by the lbvector C attempt.
Changed compiler and linker flags, notably adding -fp fmadd and switching CFLAGS processor from gekko to 750.
Adjusted linker script placement for the new C object or split assembly.
Annotated lbvector-related small data constants around lbl_804D7AA8.
Deleted monolithic text assembly file as part of a large split.
New split assembly chunk replacing part of asm/text.s.
New split assembly chunk replacing part of asm/text.s.
New split assembly chunk replacing part of asm/text.s.
New split assembly chunk replacing part of asm/text.s.
Added helper script for assembly comparison.
Added *.dump ignore pattern for generated temporary comparison artifacts.

what_changed: Attempted to replace at least the early libvector assembly range with C in src/lbvector.c.
Renamed asm/lbvector.s to asm/lbvector_asm.s and wrapped functions from func_8000D2EC through func_8000E138 in .if 0/.endif, leaving later assembly such as func_8000E19C active.
Added explanatory inline comments to lbvector assembly identifying vector element accesses such as a[0], a[1], a[2], dot products, reciprocal square-root refinement, and double-to-float conversion.
Added lbvector-associated constants labeling in asm/sdata2.s near lbl_804D7AA8.
Changed Makefile CFLAGS from '-Cpp_exceptions off -proc gekko -fp hard -O4,p' to '-Cpp_exceptions off -fp fmadd -proc 750 -O4,p' and added '-fp fmadd -proc gekko' to LDFLAGS.
Deleted the monolithic asm/text.s and added asm/text_part1.s through asm/text_part4.s, an extremely large repository layout change not directly limited to lbvector.
Added asmdiff.sh and ignored *.dump files, suggesting local binary/assembly diffing was part of the workflow.
The PR was closed without merge after the author stated they were giving up and suspected the compiler version was wrong.
smart_moves: Kept the original lbvector assembly available as asm/lbvector_asm.s instead of deleting it outright, making it possible to compare C output against the known-good assembly.
Used .if 0/.endif to disable only the replaced assembly range, a reversible WIP tactic for partial decompilation.

Annotated assembly operations with semantic vector math comments before or during decompilation, helping identify normalization, dot product, scaling, reciprocal square root, and spline/interpolation-like code patterns.
Tried compiler flag changes around fused multiply-add behavior (-fp fmadd), which is relevant for matching PowerPC floating-point code using fmadds, fmsubs, fnmsub, and related instructions.
Added an asmdiff helper and ignored dump artifacts, showing an attempt to formalize repeated matching checks.
decomp_lessons: Vector math code is highly sensitive to compiler version and floating-point codegen settings; even apparently small differences in -proc and -fp flags can make matching fail.
When matching functions with frsqrte and Newton-style refinement, preserve exact precision behavior, including double operations, frsp conversions, and fused multiply-add/subtract forms.
Inline assembly comments that map raw loads to vector fields, such as a[0]/a[1]/a[2], are useful stepping stones before naming structs or writing C.
Large unrelated asm reorganization, such as splitting asm/text.s while also decompiling a library file, can obscure the actual decompilation signal in review and future archaeology.

A WIP branch that requires suspected compiler-version changes should record the exact compiler hypothesis and observed mismatches; this PR only preserves the conclusion that the compiler version may have been wrong.

Keeping residual assembly in a renamed *_asm.s file and disabling replaced ranges is a reusable migration pattern for partial decompilation, but it should be paired with clear object/linker placement changes.

naming_conventions.meaning: C source for the lbvector/libvector module.
Residual assembly backing for lbvector after some functions are attempted in C.
Unidentified functions and constants retained address-based names; no strong evidence of final semantic function names in the provided excerpt.
Split assembly chunks for the former monolithic asm/text.s.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Compared generated code against assembly using an added asmdiff.sh helper and generated *.dump files.
Used .if 0 blocks in assembly to suppress functions believed to be replaced by C while retaining source assembly for reference.
Experimented with '-fp fmadd' to influence generation of fused floating-point instructions matching PowerPC fmadds/fmsubs/fnmsub sequences.
Experimented with processor flags, changing C compilation to '-proc 750' while linker flags included '-proc gekko'.
Assembly comments decomposed expressions instruction by instruction, e.g. dot products, vector component scaling, and reciprocal square-root calculations.
The author concluded that a compiler version mismatch might prevent matching, so this PR is evidence that compiler identity/settings should be validated before spending too long on libvector.

review_feedback.text: Giving up on this file. Someone else can try it, but I think our compiler version is wrong.
review_feedback.implication: No formal review comments were present; the main feedback is the author's own conclusion that the matching blocker may be the compiler version.

follow_up_queries: Find later merged PRs that decompiled src/lbvector.c or renamed lbvector functions semantically.
Search for subsequent compiler version or mwcc setting changes motivated by libvector matching failures.
Check whether -fp fmadd and -proc 750 were retained, reverted, or replaced in later successful matching work.
Inspect later treatment of asm/text_part1.s through asm/text_part4.s to see whether the split was accepted independently.
Compare this WIP lbvector.c against later matching implementations to identify which expressions or constants were reusable.
